steps: Triangle $QRS$ has a right angle at $R$. The labeled side $SR = 18$ is opposite angle $Q$. Since the right angle is at $R$, the hypotenuse is $QS$. The condition $QR \lt RS$ confirms $RS$ is the longer leg.
Apply the sine ratio from angle $Q$: $\sin Q = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \dfrac{SR}{QS} = \dfrac{18}{QS}$.

• Choice A: $18\cos Q$ — multiplying by cosine yields a value smaller than 18, but $QS$ is the hypotenuse and must be the longest side; incorrect.
  • Choice B: $18\sin Q$ — multiplying by sine also shrinks the value; incorrect.
  • Choice C: $\dfrac{18}{\cos Q}$ — this would hold if 18 were the adjacent side, but 18 = SR is the side opposite $Q$, not adjacent; incorrect.
  • Choice D: $\dfrac{18}{\sin Q}$ — this matches $QS = 18/\sin Q$ from the sine ratio. D is correct.
